stub: She is a Labour MP who comes from a white working class background.
constituency: She is from a constituency that is not your constituency.
personalization: She frequently speaks out and votes against her party leadership.
substantive: She opposes increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week.
justification: In political debates, she regularly explains how her decisions help to promote the interests of people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background).
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “I don’t care about making popular decisions to win elections, but about standing up for my principles and values.”
text: She is a Labour MP who comes from a white working class background. She is from a constituency that is not your constituency. She frequently speaks out and votes against her party leadership. She opposes increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week. In political debates, she regularly explains how her decisions help to promote the interests of people like you (e.g. of your class and educational background). Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “I don’t care about making popular decisions to win elections, but about standing up for my principles and values.”
